title: Alright it might be time to stop saying Evangelion
display_title: Alright it might be time to stop saying Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Alright, it might be time to stop saying ''Evangelion''             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Jul 20, 2011 1:05 am Gainax misspelled and mispronounced “Ευανγελιον”. The Greek romanisation should be “Euangelion”, which is pronounced “Eff-awn-yell-ee-ohn”. But, should we go by the Greek spelling and pronunciation, or Gainax's incorrect but intended pronunciation? I prefer the former, although purely for aesthetic reasons.

[]:             Quote Post by Yue » Wed Jul 20, 2011 1:19 am The official translation is Evangelion. If the american name used "Gospel", maybe, but nope.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jul 20, 2011 2:18 am The pronunciation of Greek has drifted a lot between classical and modern; the "yell" phoneme is the modern pronunciation, where the gamma has drifted from the original hard consonant to a much softer sound. A more dramatic example of the overall softening of consonants over the centuries is where in some classical fragments the voice of the sheep is given as BH-BH, which in modern Greek would read as "vi-vi"!

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Wed Jul 20, 2011 10:08 am I pronounce it "E-van-ghel-e-an" just for the heck of it. My bro gets so pissed cause I don't say it right.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jul 20, 2011 10:15 am Some guy on U-Tube once asked a proper linguist to stopp ppl from pronouncing it how they wanted to, like, with a soft g or with "e" like in the alphabet etc. Said linguist indicated that the "v" is actually pronounced like one... Too lazy to search for the link now, but I will if I'm asked.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jul 20, 2011 11:24 am Kendrix wrote: Some guy on U-Tube once asked a proper linguist to stopp ppl from pronouncing it how they wanted to, like, with a soft g or with "e" like in the alphabet etc. Said linguist indicated that the "v" is actually pronounced like one... Too lazy to search for the link now, but I will if I'm asked.

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Wed Jul 20, 2011 11:38 am The Eva Monkey wrote: The correct way to pronounce Evangelion is the same way it's pronounced by the characters within the actual show.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Jul 20, 2011 11:44 am ^usually it's more close to a "l" than a "r". Do you remember any scene in the show where they pronounced it with a trilling "r"?

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Wed Jul 20, 2011 11:46 am Tarnsman wrote: Evangerion?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jul 20, 2011 11:47 am Tarnsman wrote: Evangerion?

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Jul 20, 2011 11:56 am ^ though in some CMs it really sounded a lot like a "r"... but it's the series pronunciation that matters.

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Wed Jul 20, 2011 12:01 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: ^usually it's more close to a "l" than a "r". Do you remember any scene in the show where they pronounced it with a trilling "r"?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jul 20, 2011 12:23 pm

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Wed Jul 20, 2011 12:34 pm Well I thought it was pretty obvious.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Wed Jul 20, 2011 12:57 pm Tarnsman wrote: Well I thought it was pretty obvious.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Jul 20, 2011 3:29 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: The correct way to pronounce Evangelion is the same way it's pronounced by the characters within the actual show.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jul 20, 2011 4:35 pm TehDonutKing wrote: Should I call her “Release”?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Jul 20, 2011 7:32 pm TehDonutKing wrote: What about Lilith? It's quite clearly pronounced like the English word “release” in the Japanese version. The dub, most English fans, and English speakers talking about the mythological Lilith usually say “Lih-lihth”. But, in Hebrew, it's pronounced “Lee-leet”. Should I call her “Release”?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Jul 21, 2011 10:20 am In any case, most of the time, we're not talking about "Gospel", but about the show and/or the mechas, and these should be spelled how their creators named em (i.e., as shown in the opening titles), and that is. apparently, with a "v".

[]: Re: Alright, it might be time to stop saying ''Evangelion''             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Fri Jul 22, 2011 12:58 pm TehDonutKing wrote: But, should we go by the Greek spelling and pronunciation, or Gainax's incorrect but intended pronunciation?

The Greek romanisation should be “Euangelion”, which is pronounced “Eff-awn-yell-ee-ohn”. But, should we go by the Greek spelling and pronunciation, or Gainax's incorrect but int

Article: Alright it might be time to stop saying Evangelion
[]: Alright, it might be time to stop saying Evangelion             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Jul 20, 2011 1:05 am Gainax misspelled and mispronounced “Ευανγελιον”. The Greek romanisation should be “Euangelion”, which is pronounced “Eff-awn-yell-ee-ohn”. But, should we go by the Greek spelling and pronunciation, or Gainax's incorrect but intended pronunciation? I prefer the former, although purely for aesthetic reasons.

[]:             Quote Post by Yue » Wed Jul 20, 2011 1:19 am The official translation is Evangelion. If the american name used "Gospel", maybe, but nope.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jul 20, 2011 2:18 am The pronunciation of Greek has drifted a lot between classical and modern; the "yell" phoneme is the modern pronunciation, where the gamma has drifted from the original hard consonant to a much softer sound. A more dramatic example of the overall softening of consonants over the centuries is where in some classical fragments the voice of the sheep is given as BH-BH, which in modern Greek would read as "vi-vi"!

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Wed Jul 20, 2011 10:08 am I pronounce it "E-van-ghel-e-an" just for the heck of it. My bro gets so pissed cause I don't say it right.
